categories.Humectant: Draws and binds water to the skin's surface for hydration (e.g. glycerin, hyaluronic acid).
categories.Emollient: Softens and smooths skin by filling gaps between skin cells (e.g. squalane, shea butter).
categories.Occlusive: Forms a protective layer that seals in moisture and reduces water loss (e.g. petrolatum, dimethicone).
categories.Antioxidant: Neutralizes free radicals from UV and pollution to protect against premature aging (e.g. vitamin C, vitamin E).
categories.Exfoliant: Removes dead surface cells to smooth texture and unclog pores (AHAs, BHA, PHAs).
categories.AHA: Alpha-hydroxy acids — water-soluble exfoliants that work on the surface (glycolic, lactic).
categories.BHA: Beta-hydroxy acid — oil-soluble exfoliant that penetrates pores (salicylic acid).
categories.PHA: Polyhydroxy acids — gentle, hydrating next-generation exfoliants (gluconolactone).
categories.Cell-communicating: Signals skin cells to behave more youthfully, such as boosting collagen (retinoids, peptides).
categories.Anti-aging: Targets visible signs of aging like fine lines, loss of firmness and uneven tone.
categories.Skin-brightening: Fades dark spots and evens skin tone by interfering with melanin production.
categories.Skin-replenishing: Restores substances skin naturally contains to support a healthy barrier (ceramides, NMF).
categories.Soothing: Calms inflammation, redness and irritation (centella, allantoin, oat).
categories.Anti-acne: Treats or prevents breakouts by clearing pores, killing bacteria or controlling oil.
categories.Antibacterial: Reduces acne-causing or harmful bacteria on the skin.
categories.UV filter: Absorbs or reflects ultraviolet radiation to protect against sun damage (sunscreen actives).
categories.Surfactant: Cleansing agent that lifts away oil and dirt and creates foam.
categories.Cleansing: Functions to remove dirt, oil, sunscreen and makeup from the skin.
categories.Preservative: Prevents microbial growth so a product stays safe to use over time.
categories.Chelator: Binds metal ions to keep formulas stable and boost preservative efficacy.
categories.Stabilizer: Keeps a formula's texture, color or actives stable over time.
categories.Thickener: Adjusts viscosity to give products their gel, lotion or cream texture.
categories.Emulsifier: Binds oil and water together so they don't separate.
categories.Solvent: Dissolves other ingredients and adjusts how a product spreads and absorbs.
categories.pH adjuster: Used in trace amounts to set a formula's final pH.
categories.Astringent: Tightens skin and helps control excess oil.
categories.Fragrance: Adds scent for sensory appeal; a common source of irritation or allergy.
